title: Lannea stuhlmannii
intro: Lannea stuhlmannii, commonly known as Stuhlmann's quince or stuhlmann's hawthorn, is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. The plant is rich in health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lannea stuhlmannii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Lannea stuhlmannii is classified as a species of plant in the family Anacardiaceae, commonly known as the cashew or poison ivy family. This family consists of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. The genus Lannea is a group of plants that are primarily found in Africa and are characterized by their small, yellow or greenish flowers and small, red or yellow fruits. Lannea stuhlmannii is one of the species within this genus, and it is native to certain regions in Africa. The exact classification of Lannea stuhlmannii within the broader taxonomy of plants is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: Angiosperms, clade: Eudicots, clade: Rosids, order: Sapindales, family: Anacardiaceae, genus: Lannea, species: L. stuhlmannii.
